habitat: Lowland evergreen forest; secondary growth; mangroves; peatswamp forest; and wet areas
story: The White Chested Babbler (Pellorneum rostratum) is an extraordinary bird that belongs to the Pellorneidae family. It is most commonly observed in Lowland evergreen forest; secondary growth; mangroves; peatswamp forest; and wet areas, where its behaviors contribute to the ecological dynamics of the area. Listed as Near Threatened (NT) by the IUCN, this species' future depends on continued conservation action. The story of the White Chested Babbler is one of adaptation and resilience, offering valuable lessons for conservationists. This poster aims to bring the White Chested Babbler into focus, highlighting its beauty and the importance of its habitat.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
